title: Buy Schwab US Dividend Equity (SCHD) ETF only if this happens
text: The Schwab US Dividend Equity ETF (SCHD) stock price has done modestly well in the past few months. This rebound was driven by the stabilising financial sector and the general rebound in American equities.

title: Is This a New Bull Market? 3 Stocks the Smartest Investors Are Watching.

title: Bond investors show confidence in Schwab after cost-cutting
text: Investors shrugged off a slump in the shares of Charles Schwab to pile into the firm's bond offering on Tuesday, in a sign of renewed confidence in the credit fundamentals of at least some regional banks after the March banking crisis.

title: Why Schwab Stock Just Broke an 8-Day Losing Streak
text: Schwab is laying off workers and investors are nervous. But Deutsche Bank is sticking with its buy rating on Schwab amid the stock's sell-off.

title: Schwab stock outpaces financial stock rebound
text: The KBW Nasdaq Bank Index BKX, +0.70% was up 0.7% on Wednesday, on pace to snap a seven-day losing streak, according to Dow Jones Market Data. Charles Schwab SCHW, +2.35% was up 2.2% as it boosted the sector.

title: Here are 13 stocks Jim Cramer is watching, including a big retailer earnings winner
text: Here are some of the tickers on my radar for Wednesday, Aug. 23, taken directly from my reporter's notebook.

title: Schwab Q3 Trader Sentiment Survey: Recession Expectations Start to Recede as Trader Outlook Brightens in the Third Quarter
text: WESTLAKE, Texas--(BUSINESS WIRE)--After two consecutive quarters of recession anticipation, trader expectations are shifting to a more optimistic sentiment overall about the market environment. The latest Charles Schwab Trader Sentiment Survey reveals that traders are more bullish on the U.S. stock market than they were in the second quarter; for the next three months, 44% are bullish and 35% are bearish, compared to 32% bullish and 52% bearish in Q2. While most (69%) of Schwab's trader clients.

title: Charles Schwab shares fall after announcing cost-cutting plan
text: Shares of Charles Schwab fell by nearly 5% to a one-month low on Tuesday after the U.S. brokerage unveiled a cost-cutting plan that came with steep one-time charges.

title: Schwab (SCHW) to Slash Jobs & Cut Offices Space to Lower Costs
text: Schwab (SCHW) is looking to eliminate jobs and close or downsize offices to reduce expenses aimed at business streamlining efforts.

title: U.S. banks and regional lenders slide across the board as S&P is latest to downgrade ratings
text: U.S. banks and regional banks fell across the board on Tuesday, after S&P Global Ratings downgraded five smaller players after a review of risk related to funding, liquidity and asset quality with a focus on office commercial real estate.

title: Why Charles Schwab Stock Is Down Today
text: Schwab said it was cutting an undisclosed number of jobs and looking to downsize its real estate footprint. The company has been hit hard by rising rates, and has had to lean into high-cost sources for funding.

title: Charles Schwab: A Discounted Behemoth, Buy The Earnings Contraction
text: Charles Schwab experienced strong long-term growth after shifting towards a banking-style business model and offering commission-free trading. Schwab faces the risk of deposit flight as customers seek higher-yield savings accounts and liquidity concerns arise, but this risk is being overestimated by the market. Schwab's strong capital buffers should give investors comfort that this company will continue compounding over time.

title: Charles Schwab slashing jobs, offices to streamline operations
text: The Charles Schwabe Corporation reported in an SEC filing Monday it plans to close or downsize some corporate offices and reduce its headcount to cut costs.

title: Charles Schwab plans to cut jobs, close offices as downturn hits Wall Street
text: Schwab has had to turn to supplementary funding sources to counter an uncertain economic environment.

title: Schwab plans ‘lower headcount,' office closings to save $500 million
text: Charles Schwab Corp. SCHW, -0.20% late Monday said it plans to reduce costs to the tune of $500 million a year through a “lower” employee headcount and professional services, without providing more details. The company also plans to close or downsize some yet unnamed corporate offices, according to a filing with securities regulators.

title: Al Gore's Firm Bet on AI and Sold Salesforce, Mastercard, Schwab Stock
text: Generation Investment, chaired by former Vice President Al Gore, bought Applied Materials stock, and sold Salesforce, Mastercard, and Schwab stock in the second quarter.

title: Acquisitions to Aid Schwab's (SCHW) Top Line Amid Cost Woes
text: Schwab's (SCHW) inorganic growth efforts are expected to be earnings accretive. However, these will lead to an increase in expenses in the near term, thus hurting profits.

title: Schwab's (SCHW) July Net New Assets Fall Amid TDA Integration
text: Schwab's (SCHW) July 2023 core net new assets decline. The main reason for lower new asset flows is the attrition of TD Ameritrade retail and advisory clients.

title: Charles Schwab: The Perpetual Growth Machine
text: Charles Schwab demonstrated continued growth with an increase in client assets to an all-time high of $8.24 trillion. Net new assets for the month came in at $12.9 billion, with an adjusted reading of $13.7 billion. The company added 303,000 new brokerage accounts in July, maintaining a trend of new accounts exceeding 300,000 for the 9th consecutive month.

title: Bank Stock Posing Attractive Entry Point
text: Charles Schwab Corporation Common Stock (NYSE:SCHW) was last seen down 2.2% to trade at $60.43, as bank stocks take a hit following a dire warning.

title: Charles Schwab Is Still Losing TD Ameritrade Clients
text: Client attrition after the fallout of the merger between Charles Schwab and TD Ameritrade is still in-line or slightly better than the company's predictions for the year, but Charles Schwab stock continues to disappoint on the charts despite earnings beats.

title: Schwab says attrition rates from Ameritrade acquisition in-line or slightly better than estimates
text: Charles Schwab Corp. SCHW, -2.84% said Monday the attrition of clients as part of its 2019 acquisition of Ameritrade “will be in-line with or slightly better than our initial estimates.” About 4% of Ameritrade revenue prior to the deal or around 1% of combined total client assets as of Dec. 31 was its attrition estimate, the financial firm said.

title: Cryptocurrencies Rally Post Banking Crisis: 3 Stocks to Buy
text: Cryptocurrencies surged on Aug 8 following concerns over the health of the nation's banks, which is likely to help stocks like Riot Platforms (RIOT), NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA), Coinbase Global (COIN).

title: Federman & Sherwood Investigates TD Ameritrade, Inc. and Charles Schwab & Co., Inc. a TD Ameritrade affiliate for Data Breach
text: OKLAHOMA CITY--(BUSINESS WIRE)---- $SCHW #CharlesSchwab--The law firm of Federman & Sherwood has initiated an investigation into TD Ameritrade, Inc. & Charles Schwab & Co., Inc. a TD Ameritrade affiliate (“the Companies”) with respect to their recent data breach. On June 26, 2023, The Companies informed clients that the company experienced a data breach after an unauthorized party accessed sensitive consumer data entrusted to the company. text: After the year-to-date rally, the overall market is approaching overvalued territory. However, the financial sector offers value after a disappointing first half.

title: 2 Top Brokerage Stocks to Buy in August
text: Brokerage stocks can benefit from periods of economic growth and market volatility. Charles Schwab struggled with deposit outflows, but its cheap valuation makes it an intriguing value stock.

title: My Top 15 High Growth Dividend Stocks For August 2023
